sections.title: Deduction in respect of life insurance premia, deferred annuity, contributions to provident fund, subscription to certain equity shares or debentures, etc.
sections.description: Allows a deduction up to ₹1,50,000 for various investments and expenses including EPF, PPF, ELSS mutual funds, LIC premiums, home loan principal repayment, and school tuition fees for children.

sections.title: Deduction in respect of health insurance premia
sections.description: Deduction for medical insurance premiums paid for self, family, and parents. Standard limit is ₹25,000 for self/family and an additional ₹25,000 for parents. If parents are senior citizens (aged 60+), the limit is ₹50,000. If the taxpayer is also a senior citizen, the limit is ₹50,000 for self/family, making the maximum deduction ₹1,00,000.
sections.eligibility: Individuals paying health insurance premium, preventive health checkup cost (up to ₹5,000), or medical expenditure of senior citizens with no health insurance.

sections.title: Deduction in respect of voluntary contribution to National Pension System (NPS)
sections.description: An additional deduction of up to ₹50,000 is allowed for contributions made to the NPS (Tier-1 account). This deduction is over and above the ₹1,50,000 limit of Section 80C.
sections.eligibility: Individual taxpayers who make voluntary contributions to NPS Tier-1.

sections.title: Deduction in respect of interest on loan taken for higher education
sections.description: Allows deduction of interest paid on loans taken for higher education of self, spouse, children, or a student for whom the individual is a legal guardian. There is no upper limit on the interest amount that can be claimed.
sections.eligibility: Interest paid on loan taken from financial institutions/approved charitable institutions. Available for 8 years or until interest is fully paid.

sections.title: Deductions in respect of donations to certain funds, charitable institutions, etc.
sections.description: Allows a tax deduction for donations made to specified charitable organizations and relief funds. Deductions are either 50% or 100% of the donated amount, with or without restrictions (typically capped at 10% of Adjusted Gross Total Income). Cash donations exceeding ₹2,000 are not eligible.
sections.eligibility: All taxpayers making donations via digital modes or bank drafts/cheques to registered 80G entities.

sections.title: Deduction in respect of interest on loan taken for certain house property
sections.description: An additional deduction of up to ₹1,50,000 for interest on home loans taken by first-time home buyers. The loan must have been sanctioned between April 1, 2019, and March 31, 2023. The stamp duty value of the residential property must not exceed ₹45 Lakh.
sections.eligibility: First-time buyers. Must not own any other residential property on the date of loan sanction.

sections.title: Deduction in respect of interest on deposits in savings account
sections.description: Deduction of up to ₹10,000 on interest earned from savings bank accounts with banks, co-operative societies, or post offices. Not applicable to senior citizens who qualify for Section 80TTB.
sections.eligibility: Individual (under 60 years) and HUF. Applies to savings account interest only.

sections.title: Deduction in respect of interest on deposits in case of senior citizens
sections.description: A higher deduction of up to ₹50,000 on interest earned from all deposits (savings bank accounts, fixed deposits, recurring deposits) with banks, co-operative societies, or post offices for resident senior citizens.
sections.eligibility: Resident senior citizens aged 60 years and above at any time during the financial year.

sections.title: Deduction in case of a person with disability
sections.description: Flat deduction for resident individuals certified with a physical disability (including blindness) or mental retardation. Standard disability (>= 40%): ₹75,000. Severe disability (>= 80%): ₹1,25,000.
sections.eligibility: Resident individuals with a valid medical certificate from a government medical authority.

sections.title: Deduction on home loan interest for self-occupied or rented property
sections.description: Deduction on the interest component of home loan repayment. For self-occupied property, the limit is ₹2,00,000. For rented out property, there is no limit, but the net loss under 'Income from House Property' that can be set off against other heads of income is capped at ₹2,00,000 per year.
sections.eligibility: Homeowners paying interest on home loan.

sections.title: House Rent Allowance Exemption
sections.description: Exemption on House Rent Allowance received by salaried employees. The exempt amount is the minimum of three values: (1) Actual HRA received, (2) Rent paid minus 10% of basic salary, (3) 50% of basic salary for metro cities (Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai) or 40% for non-metro cities.
sections.eligibility: Salaried individuals who live in rented accommodation and receive HRA.
